metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test asserting that `--check` exits non-zero (or raises SystemExit with code != 0) when invoked against a temp dir whose synthetic `.py` contains `os.environ["REBAR_FAKE_NEW"]` but the committed/reference doc does NOT yet list `REBAR_FAKE_NEW`.
findings.finding: The testing plan verifies that `--check` exits 0 on the committed tree, but never tests that `--check` exits non-zero when the committed doc is STALE (i.e., the live scan would produce a diff). This is the primary failure mode of the CI drift gate — if `--check`'s exit-code-on-drift logic is broken, the gate silently passes on real drift.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — DRIFT bullet: '`--check` against the committed tree exits 0'
findings.suggested_fix: In `tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py`, after the existing drift-detectability assertion, add a case: write a synthetic `.py` with `os.environ["REBAR_FAKE_NEW"]` to a temp dir, write a 'committed doc' that omits `REBAR_FAKE_NEW`, then assert `gen_env_registry.main(['--check', '--root', str(tmp), '--committed', str(stale_doc)])` raises SystemExit with a non-zero code (or returns a truthy exit code).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the docs-pointer target: either specify that `docs/README.md` must be CREATED as part of this plan, or name an existing docs index file (e.g. `docs/config.md`) where the `docs/env-vars.md` link will be added.
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` does not exist in the repository; the plan names it as an existing edit target ('A pointer to `docs/env-vars.md` in the `docs/README.md` index') and AC5 requires it to link `docs/env-vars.md`. The `docs/` directory contains no `README.md`, so this is a file-creation task, not an edit — and the AC cannot be verified against a file that doesn't exist.
findings.location: What / files — last bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `docs/README.md` with an existing docs index file (e.g. `docs/config.md` or the root `README.md`) as the pointer location, or explicitly scope creation of `docs/README.md` as a deliverable.
findings.finding: docs/README.md is named as an existing edit target ('A pointer to docs/env-vars.md in the docs/README.md index') and AC5 requires it to link env-vars.md, but this file does not exist in the repo — the docs/ directory contains no README.md.
findings.location: What / files — 'A pointer to docs/env-vars.md in the docs/README.md index (Operator section)'; AC5: 'docs/README.md links docs/env-vars.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'A pointer to docs/env-vars.md in the docs/README.md index' to 'A pointer to docs/env-vars.md in docs/config.md (Operator section)', and update AC5 to match.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the alias-filter description: replace `via="env"` with `kind == "env"` (the actual `Deprecation.kind` field) in the pattern-3 description so the generator is implemented correctly.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the generator to filter `_deprecations.py REGISTRY` using `via="env"` entries, but the `Deprecation` dataclass has no `via` attribute — the real discriminator field is `kind`. The correct filter is `d.kind == "env"` (or equivalently `d.key.startswith("env:")`). A generator written to match `via=="env"` will either crash with AttributeError or silently emit zero alias rows.
findings.suggested_fix: Change plan text to: 'from `src/rebar/_deprecations.py` REGISTRY (`kind=="env"` entries: …)'; and in the generator use `[d for d in _deprecations.REGISTRY.values() if d.kind == "env"]`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `via="env"` with `kind == "env"` in the plan's description of the _deprecations.py REGISTRY filter (the Deprecation dataclass uses `kind`, not `via`).
findings.finding: The plan instructs gen_env_registry.py to filter _deprecations.py REGISTRY using `via="env"` entries, but the Deprecation dataclass has no `via` field — the actual discriminator field is `kind`. An executor following the plan literally will get an AttributeError or silently emit nothing.
findings.location: What / files — 'Alias/deprecation status — from src/rebar/_deprecations.py REGISTRY (via="env" entries)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'via="env" entries' to 'kind=="env" entries' throughout the plan and in any code guidance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix Pattern 3 to filter `_deprecations.REGISTRY` by `d.kind == "env"` (not `d.via == "env"`) — `via` is the emission-channel kwarg on `warn_deprecated()`, not a `Deprecation` field.
findings.finding: Pattern 3 says it reads `_deprecations.py REGISTRY (via="env" entries)`, but the `Deprecation` dataclass has a `kind` field, not a `via` field. `via` is only the emission-channel parameter of `warn_deprecated()`. Code filtering `d.via == "env"` will raise `AttributeError` at runtime; the correct filter is `d.kind == "env"`.
findings.location: What/files section — Pattern 3 description: '_deprecations.py REGISTRY (via="env" entries)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'via="env" entries' with 'kind=="env" entries' in the plan, and in the generator code filter: `[d for d in REGISTRY.values() if d.kind == 'env']`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct `via="env"` to `kind == "env"` (i.e., filter `_deprecations.py REGISTRY` by `d.kind == 'env'`) in the What/files item 3 and any generated doc header language that copies this phrasing.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the generator to read alias/deprecation status 'from _deprecations.py REGISTRY (via="env" entries)', but the `Deprecation` dataclass has no `via` field — the actual discriminator is `kind` (e.g., `kind == "env"` or REGISTRY key prefix `"env:"`). An executor implementing the generator as written would fail with AttributeError or silently include all REGISTRY entries regardless of kind.
findings.location: What / files — item 3 (Alias/deprecation status): 'from `src/rebar/_deprecations.py` REGISTRY (`via="env"` entries')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `via="env" entries` with `kind=="env" entries` (i.e., filter by `d.kind == "env"`) in the plan's alias/deprecation section and in the doc-header enumeration it references.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended format of `docs/env-vars.md` in the plan (e.g. markdown table with columns: Variable, Default, Source file(s), Alias/deprecated-for, Description; rows sorted alphabetically; optional top-level grouping by module prefix).
findings.finding: The output format (schema) of `docs/env-vars.md` is never specified — no mention of columns (e.g. variable name, default value, source module(s), alias/deprecation status, description), row ordering, or section grouping. An executor must invent this, and AC1's drift gate will lock in whatever they choose, making a later format improvement a breaking drift change.
findings.location: ## What / files → docs/env-vars.md bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `docs/env-vars.md` bullet: 'Generated as a markdown table with columns `| Variable | Default | Source file(s) | Notes |`, rows sorted alphabetically, deprecated vars annotated inline; a leading header block lists the scanned patterns verbatim.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split AC5 into three separate acceptance criteria: one for the drift-gate step in test.yml, one for the docs/README.md pointer, and one for `make lint` green.
findings.finding: AC5 bundles three independently verifiable outcomes into a single criterion: (a) drift-gate step exists in test.yml, (b) docs/README.md links docs/env-vars.md, and (c) `make lint` is green. If any one sub-item fails it is unclear which outcome is incomplete, and CI cannot report partial success per criterion.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace AC5 with:
- [ ] A 'Env-var registry drift gate' step is present in `.github/workflows/test.yml` and runs `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py --check`
- [ ] `docs/README.md` (Operator section) contains a link to `docs/env-vars.md`
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0 on the branch

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update Pattern 3 to enumerate all 6 `kind='env'` REGISTRY aliases, and add at least one more alias assertion to the unit test (e.g. `REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT` appearing with its `→REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT` annotation).
findings.finding: Pattern 3 only mentions 2 of the 6 actual `kind='env'` aliases in `_deprecations.py` REGISTRY, and AC3 only asserts `REBAR_NO_SYNC` appears with an alias annotation — leaving `SCRATCH_BASE_DIR→REBAR_SCRATCH_BASE_DIR`, `REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT→REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT`, `RECONCILER_ABSENT_GET_BUDGET→REBAR_RECONCILER_DELETION_PROBE_LIMIT`, and `REBAR_ID_GUARD_MODE→REBAR_UNSAFE_ID_GUARD_BYPASS` untested. If the generator silently drops any of them, no test catches it.
findings.location: What/files section — Pattern 3 enumeration; AC line: 'ALIAS assertions (same test): REBAR_NO_SYNC appears with its _deprecations alias annotation'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Pattern 4 to `gen_env_registry.py`: import `_SECTIONS` and `_CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES` from `rebar._config_schema`/`rebar.config` at scan time and enumerate all `(sect, key)` pairs to derive their canonical env names — capturing the `env_overrides()` class of vars in the main registry rather than the dynamic/unresolved section.
findings.finding: The config system's `env_overrides()` in `config.py` reads ALL canonical config env vars (`REBAR_<SECTION>_<KEY>`, e.g. `REBAR_SYNC_PULL`, `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH`, `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`, `REBAR_COMPACT_THRESHOLD`, `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_SIGNATURE_FOR_CLOSE`, etc.) by iterating `_SECTIONS` and calling `os.environ[_canonical_env_name(sect, key)]` — not via string-literal calls. Pattern 1 misses all of them; no helper in `KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS` covers them. They land in 'dynamic/unresolved' even though they are fully enumerable at import time from `_SECTIONS` + `_CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES`. The plan does not mention this gap, so a large systematic class of operator-facing env vars is silently absent from the main registry section of the generated doc.
findings.location: What/files section — Pattern 1 scope; generator design
findings.suggested_fix: In the generator, after Pattern 1-3 collection, add: `from rebar._config_schema import _SECTIONS; from rebar.config import _CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES; for sect, keys in _SECTIONS.items(): for key in keys: name = _CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES.get((sect, key), f"REBAR_{sect.upper()}_{key.upper()}"); registry.add(name, source='config-schema', section=sect, key=key)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py` as an explicit entry in the 'What/files' section so all deliverables are enumerated in one place.
findings.finding: The unit test file `tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py` is referenced in three ACs (AC2, AC3, AC4) and listed as in-scope, but is never enumerated as a deliverable in the 'What/files' section — leaving an uncovered described deliverable gap.
findings.location: What/files section (omission) vs. AC2, AC3, AC4 and Scope ('a unit test')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to 'What/files': `- tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py`: unit tests covering positive capture (AC2), alias assertions (AC3), and drift detectability (AC4).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to CLAUDE.md (or CONTRIBUTING / the script's own top-of-file docstring if that is the canonical dev-guide location) explaining that any new env-read helper must be registered in KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS in scripts/gen_env_registry.py, with a one-line example.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new contributor contract — adding a row to KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS whenever a new env-read helper is created — but does not update CLAUDE.md (or equivalent developer-facing guidance) to document this maintenance workflow. A contributor adding a new helper function will not know the registry will silently miss their reads until CI fails with a confusing drift error.
findings.location: ## What / files — KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS table; ## Scope — 'extend by adding a row'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'files' list, add: 'CLAUDE.md (or CONTRIBUTING): a one-paragraph "Env-var registry" section noting that new env-read helper functions must be added to KNOWN_ENV_HELPERS in scripts/gen_env_registry.py; include a one-line example row.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `gen_env_registry.py` writes `docs/env-vars.md` atomically (write to a `.tmp` sibling file, then `os.replace()` to the target) so an interrupted run never leaves a partial/corrupt committed artifact.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify that `docs/env-vars.md` is written atomically (e.g., write-to-temp-then-rename). If `gen_env_registry.py` is interrupted mid-write, the committed file is left in a partial/corrupt state, requiring manual `git checkout` recovery.
findings.suggested_fix: In the generator's write path: `tmp = target.with_suffix('.tmp'); tmp.write_text(content); tmp.replace(target)` — `os.replace` is atomic on POSIX and win32.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py` as an explicit entry in the 'What / files' deliverables list.
findings.finding: tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py is cited in three ACs (AC2, AC3, AC4) and listed as in-scope in the Scope section, but is never enumerated as a deliverable in the 'What / files' section, making it an unenumerated required artifact.
findings.location: What / files section (omits the test file); AC2, AC3, AC4 (reference tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py); Scope ('a unit test')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to 'What / files': '- `tests/unit/test_gen_env_registry.py`: unit test covering positive capture (AC2), alias assertions (AC3), and drift detectability (AC4).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
